message:
tests: proptest coverage for BoxedQuad round-trip + HybridSearchBundle

Adds two new property-based test suites filling coverage gaps found
during a grep sweep across crates/frankenterm-core/tests/ and
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/quad.rs:

1. BoxedQuad round-trip (gui/src/quad.rs).

The BoxedQuad <-> Vec<Vertex> conversion is a hot path that packs
seven per-vertex fields (position, tex, fg_color, alt_color, hsv,
has_color, mix_value) into a structure-of-arrays layout suitable
for the webgpu pipeline. Until now only a handful of hand-written
unit tests covered it.

The new proptest generates arbitrary four-vertex arrays (one per
quad corner) across the realistic value ranges the renderer uses:
  - position coords in -10k..10k (screen space, well past wraps)
  - tex coords in -4..4 (atlas addressing with repeat modes)
  - colour channels in 0..1 (premultiplied-alpha invariant)
  - hsv shifts in -2..2 (signed rotation)
  - has_color flag in -4..8 (covers every discriminant case)
  - mix_value in -1..2

Then asserts `BoxedQuad::from_vertices(v).to_vertices() == v` as
a structural identity.  Any regression that perturbs SOA packing,
field ordering, or float NaN handling fails this test with a
minimised counter-example rather than a silent rendering glitch.

Adds `proptest = "1.8.0"` as a dev-dependency of frankenterm-gui
and regenerates Cargo.lock.

2. HybridSearchBundle + HybridSearchResult (new test file).

Covers the storage-layer types returned by
`StorageHandle::hybrid_search_with_results` which previously had
zero proptest coverage anywhere in the tree. The new test file
exercises Segment/SearchResult/HybridSearchResult construction
under arbitrary i64/u64 ids, arbitrary UTF-8 content, arbitrary
optional 8-64-char hex hashes, and arbitrary captured-at timestamps
- matching the real value ranges the storage handle returns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -126,6 +126,7 @@ windows = { workspace = true, features = [
 [dev-dependencies]
 benchmarking = "0.4.13"
 k9.workspace = true
+proptest = "1.8.0"
 
 # NOTE: Workspace lints intentionally omitted - vendored GUI code carries
 # upstream style that does not conform to our pedantic clippy profile.
